The reason I'm not asking for segment access, just to, like, make this clear, unless segment has changed since I've been hands-on with it, which is certainly possible, think of what segment is doing as largely, like, connecting pipes together of different systems, and then the people moving through those pipes, they're creating attributes, reproducing IDs, around?
Milpl

And so the utility for, like, when I was at Trey, for example, is, like, we had Segment across all our platform, like, all our web properties.
And when we would think about, like, hey, I wonder if we can measure this or what happened in this situation, whatever it may be.
A lot of times it would come back to, like, oh, well, there's going to be an ID associated to that person.
There's going to be attributes tied to that ID, such as, like, the referring domain that they came in from, whether that's Google or ChatGPT or whatever it may be.
And that we could use that to work back and create reports off of that data to, like, work back to what happened.
Segment, though, is not the place that you're building those reports and doing that.
It is the thing that is helping provide you the foundation to do that reporting elsewhere more often than not.
